trans-unit.source: QGIS Exercise 2: Understanding the user interface and getting to know the layer concept
trans-unit.target: QGIS Exercise 2: Understanding the user interface and getting to know the layer concept

trans-unit.source: This exercise expands on the previous exercise. This time, we will also import data into a QGIS-project. We will learn how to display vector data in QGIS, view the attribute table, as well as learn how to reproject layers. This exercise touches upon content from module 2 and is aimed to refresh your knowledge about QGIS if you have already created your first projects.
trans-unit.target: This exercise expands on the previous exercise. This time, we will also import data into a QGIS-project. We will learn how to display vector data in QGIS, view the attribute table, as well as learn how to reproject layers. This exercise touches upon content from module 2 and is aimed to refresh your knowledge about QGIS if you have already created your first projects.

trans-unit.source: Download the data (zip-file: 83.23 KB) and save it on your PC. Create a local folder and save the above data there. (.zip folders must be unzipped beforehand.)
trans-unit.target: Download the data (zip-file: 83.23 KB) and save it on your PC. Create a local folder and save the above data there. (.zip folders must be unzipped beforehand.)

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS and familiarise yourself with the user interface.
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS and familiarise yourself with the user interface.

trans-unit.source: Open the above files in QGIS. Load the vector layers into your program. Load the CSV file via `Add delimited text`.
trans-unit.target: Open the above files in QGIS. Load the vector layers into your program. Load the CSV file via `Add delimited text`.

trans-unit.source: Interact with the map and explore the data sets. Use the zoom tool and move the map. Notice the status bar at the bottom of the screen and how it changes.
trans-unit.target: Interact with the map and explore the data sets. Use the zoom tool and move the map. Notice the status bar at the bottom of the screen and how it changes.

trans-unit.source: Familiarise yourself with the layer window (Layer List). Alternately show and hide different layers and move the layers in the hierarchy. Rename the data layer in a meaningful way. Note that the latter has no effect on the data sources (file names, storage location).
trans-unit.target: Familiarise yourself with the layer window (Layer List). Alternately show and hide different layers and move the layers in the hierarchy. Rename the data layer in a meaningful way. Note that the latter has no effect on the data sources (file names, storage location).

trans-unit.source: View the attribute data of the layers. For this purpose look at the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: View the attribute data of the layers. For this purpose look at the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: Changes the projection in the map view to WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator- EPSG:3857. Note that this does not change the projection (the coordinates) of the files, but only affects the projection of the map view. Check this in the properties of the point layer. Which projection is indicated there? Hint: You can use the search-bar on the top.
trans-unit.target: Changes the projection in the map view to WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator- EPSG:3857. Note that this does not change the projection (the coordinates) of the files, but only affects the projection of the map view. Check this in the properties of the point layer. Which projection is indicated there? Hint: You can use the search-bar on the top.

trans-unit.source: Now save the health layer in the projection WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator- EPSG:3857. This changes the projection of the file. Check this in the properties of the newly created layer.
trans-unit.target: Now save the health layer in the projection WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator- EPSG:3857. This changes the projection of the file. Check this in the properties of the newly created layer.

trans-unit.source: Optional: You can add the OpenStreetMap base map via the browser window => XYZ Tiles. Note that online base maps can lead to problems with the projection of the different layers. Make sure to remove the base map before saving the project.
trans-unit.target: Optional: You can add the OpenStreetMap base map via the browser window => XYZ Tiles. Note that online base maps can lead to problems with the projection of the different layers. Make sure to remove the base map before saving the project.

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} This (or similar) is what it looks like in the end: ![QGIS_User_Interface](/fig/en_eExercise_1_result.png) :::
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} This (or similar) is what it looks like in the end: ![QGIS_User_Interface](/fig/en_eExercise_1_result.png) :::
